title: Paraderris cuneifolia
taxonomy: Paraderris cuneifolia is a species of plant in the family Annonaceae, commonly known as the custard-apple or soursop family. It is native to Australia, specifically in the tropical and subtropical regions. Paraderris cuneifolia belongs to the genus Paraderris, which consists of several species of flowering plants. This plant is a type of evergreen shrub or small tree that grows up to a certain height and has distinctive leaves and flowers. Paraderris cuneifolia is classified as a member of the flowering plant kingdom, specifically in the class Magnoliopsida.
